questions.subject: Psychiatry
questions.stem: A 58-year-old man with severe depression insists that his bowels have rotted away and that he is already dead. He has stopped eating. Which delusion is this?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Capgras = belief a familiar person has been replaced by an identical impostor.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Cotard = nihilistic delusion of being dead, not existing, or having rotting/absent organs. Classically seen in severe (psychotic) depression.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Fregoli = belief that different people are in fact one persecutor in disguise.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Othello = delusional jealousy (belief partner is unfaithful).

choices.label: E
choices.explanation: Ekbom = delusional infestation (belief of being infested with parasites).

questions.keyPoint: Cotard = 'I am dead / my organs have rotted.' A nihilistic delusion, red flag for severe psychotic depression and high suicide risk.
questions.keyDifferentiator: Cotard (I am dead) vs Capgras (impostor) vs Fregoli (one disguised persecutor).

rows: Strangers are one persecutor in disguise

questions.subject: Psychiatry
questions.stem: Hours after his first dose of haloperidol, a young man develops a sustained upward gaze and a twisted, rigid neck. He is distressed but alert. What is the immediate treatment?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: This is acute dystonia (oculogyric crisis + torticollis). An anticholinergic (procyclidine/benztropine) reverses it rapidly.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Propranolol treats akathisia, not acute dystonia.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Dantrolene is for neuroleptic malignant syndrome (hyperthermia + lead-pipe rigidity), not isolated dystonia.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Stopping helps eventually, but the patient is distressed — give an anticholinergic now.

choices.label: E
choices.explanation: Dangerous — would worsen the extrapyramidal reaction.

questions.keyPoint: Acute dystonia (early, dramatic, oculogyric crisis/torticollis) → IM anticholinergic (procyclidine) for rapid relief.

questions.subject: Psychiatry
questions.stem: A patient on an SSRI is also taking tramadol. He becomes agitated and febrile with hyperreflexia, clonus and tremor. Which is the diagnosis?

choices.label: A
choices.explanation: Serotonergic drug combination + the triad of neuromuscular excitation (clonus, hyperreflexia), autonomic instability and altered mental state = serotonin syndrome. Onset is rapid (hours).

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: NMS follows dopamine blockers and is hypokinetic — lead-pipe rigidity, hyporeflexia, bradykinesia — evolving over days.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Gives dry skin, urinary retention and absent bowel sounds — not clonus/hyperreflexia.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Triggered by volatile anaesthetics/suxamethonium, not SSRIs.

choices.label: E
choices.explanation: Coarse tremor, ataxia and confusion — not the hyper-reflexic clonus picture, and no lithium here.

questions.keyPoint: Serotonin syndrome is HYPERkinetic (clonus, hyperreflexia, rapid onset). NMS is HYPOkinetic (rigidity, hyporeflexia, slow onset).
questions.keyDifferentiator: Clonus + hyperreflexia → serotonin syndrome. Lead-pipe rigidity + hyporeflexia → NMS.

questions.subject: Psychiatry
questions.stem: Which factor most strongly increases the risk of completed suicide following an act of deliberate self-harm?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Detailed planning with precautions against discovery
choices.explanation: High suicidal intent — planning, a final act (e.g. a note/will), and steps to avoid being found — is the strongest predictor of a completed suicide.

choices.label: B
choices.explanation: Repeated low-lethality self-harm signals distress but is not the strongest predictor of completion.

choices.label: C
choices.explanation: Impulsivity carries risk, but planned, concealed attempts with high intent carry more.

choices.label: D
choices.explanation: Women attempt more often; men have higher completion rates.

choices.label: E
choices.explanation: Social support is generally protective.

questions.keyPoint: It's the INTENT that predicts risk: planning, precautions against discovery, a final act, and ongoing wish to die.
questions.keyDifferentiator: Frequency of self-harm ≠ lethality. A single highly-planned, concealed attempt outweighs repeated superficial acts.
